428 J Street r Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
September 7,2004
Tom Sykes, City Administrator
City of Commerce
CityHall
2535 Commerce Way
Commerce, CA 90040
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-04-186
Dear Mr. Sykes:
This letter confirms the receipt of your request for advice regarding an unnamed elected
council member. Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(bX8XB),
unless you identifu the official and are writing as hislher authorized representativg the Fair
Political Practices Commission can neither address your questions norprovide analysis. The
FPPC may also decline to provide wriuen advice, where an inquiry is hypothetical in nature
(regulation I 8329OX8XD).
I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review on the requirements for
submitting an advice request. I have also included regulation18702.5 @ublic Identification of a
Conflict of Interest for Section 87200 Filers) and Limitations and Restrictions on Gifts,
Honoraria, Travel and Loans for locally elected officials; these documents may address some
your concerns.
Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-566A.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel
W. Wallace
Enclosures
JwWjg
Assistant General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: A City of Commerce administrator requested FPPC advice regarding an unnamed elected council member's conduct under the Political Reform Act, but failed to identify the specific official or provide authorization to act on their behalf.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request did not identify the specific elected official in question and was not submitted by an authorized representative, as required by California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B). The FPPC also noted the request appeared hypothetical in nature, which is grounds for declination under regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination letter with no formal question or conclusion sections. Reasons for declination clearly stated in body text citing specific regulatory violations. Enclosures provided for requestor guidance on proper submission requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A City of Commerce administrator requested FPPC advice regarding an unnamed elected council member's conduct under the Political Reform Act, but failed to identify the specific official or provide authorization to act on their behalf.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the request did not identify the specific elected official in question and was not submitted by an authorized representative, as required by California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B). The FPPC also noted the request appeared hypothetical in nature, which is grounds for declination under regulation 18329(b)(8)(D).

Under the provisions of California Code of Regulations section 18329(bX8XB), unless you identifu the official and are writing as hislher authorized representativg the Fair Political Practices Commission can neither address your questions norprovide analysis. The FPPC may also decline to provide wriuen advice, where an inquiry is hypothetical in nature (regulation I 8329OX8XD). I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review on the requirements for submitting an advice request. I have also included regulation18702.5 @ublic Identification of a Conflict of Interest for Section 87200 Filers) and Limitations and Restrictions on Gifts, Honoraria, Travel and Loans for locally elected officials; these documents may address some your concerns. Should you have any questions, please contact me at (916) 322-566A. Sincerely, Luisa Menchaca Counsel W. Wallace Enclosures JwWjg Assistant General Counsel
embedding.summary: The FPPC declined an advice request from the City of Commerce regarding an unnamed council member's potential conflict of interest, citing failure to identify the specific official and lack of authorized representation as required by regulatory provisions.
